mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and equity valuations upward over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 PAYEMS at 134,061k suggests labor market stabilization post-2008
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following months with PAYEMS growth > 150k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 3.5% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates on future corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 FEDFUNDS at 3.78% indicates active tightening stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated policy rates
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as growth forecasts are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 RSAFS at 351,561M reflects post-crisis demand recovery
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with demand deceleration signals
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) rising above 107 signals strong manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 INDPRO at 107.055 indicates post-recession industrial recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with expanding industrial output
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of months with INDPRO > 107, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly declining signals broad-based demand weakness across residential and consumer sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as recession risk rises.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 HOUST at 2,108k and RSAFS at 351,561M show stabilization but vulnerability to joint shocks
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand deterioration across housing and retail
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when both triggers are met, or joint Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.2% combined with declining payroll growth signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 UNRATE at 5.0% and PAYEMS at 134,061k show labor market near inflection point
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deterioration with synchronized employment weakness
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or joint Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 200 index points combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 3.5%) signals stagflationary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real returns compress.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 CPIAUCSL at 198.5 and FEDFUNDS at 3.78% indicate inflation-fighting monetary stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or joint Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% annualized rate signals weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2011 GDPC1 at 11,193.2B reflects post-crisis recovery deceleration
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating GDP growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of quarters with GDPC1 growth < 0.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
